tool_args.raw_text: Bridges and Tunnels Key Performance Metrics - December 2025

payload.title: Bridges and Tunnels Key Performance Metrics
payload.description: Key performance metrics document prepared for the December 2025 meeting of the Bridges and Tunnels Committee

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: Catherine Sheridan, President of Bridges and Tunnels
tool_args.raw_text: Catherine Sheridan President, Bridges and Tunnels

tool_args.label: MTA Bridges and Tunnels
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Bridges and Tunnels is comprised of over 1,000 employees and operates seven bridges and two tunnels in New York City, handling over 330 million vehicle crossings every year in addition to managing the Congestion Relief Zone.

payload.description: Operates seven bridges and two tunnels in New York City, handling over 330 million vehicle crossings every year in addition to managing the Congestion Relief Zone

warnings: entity.parent_entity is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Parent entities are relation context. Suggested relation: part_of_agency.

tool_args.label: Metropolitan Transportation Authority
tool_args.raw_text: The Metropolitan Transportation Authority is North America's largest transportation network, serving a population of 15.3 million people across a 5,000 square-mile travel area surrounding New York City through Long Island, southeastern New York State, and Connecticut.

payload.description: North America's largest transportation network, serving a population of 15.3 million people across a 5,000 square-mile travel area surrounding New York City through Long Island, southeastern New York State, and Connecticut

tool_args.label: MTA Construction and Development
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Construction and Development is in the early stages of a $34 million contract to rehabilitate the Manhattan entrance of the Hugh L. Carey Tunnel

tool_args.label: Rehabilitation of Hugh L. Carey Tunnel Manhattan Plaza
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Construction and Development is in the early stages of a $34 million contract to rehabilitate the Manhattan entrance of the Hugh L. Carey Tunnel (HLCT, formerly known as the Brooklyn-Battery Tunnel). The contract was awarded in September 2025 and is scheduled to be substantially complete by December 2026.

payload.project_name: Rehabilitation of Hugh L. Carey Tunnel Manhattan Plaza
payload.description: $34 million contract to rehabilitate the Manhattan entrance of the Hugh L. Carey Tunnel, including replacement of electrical and communications systems, structural and retaining wall repairs, paving and striping, drainage improvements, and replacement of over-height detection system sensors and IVIS tolling loop

warnings: project.lead_agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Lead agency is relation context. Suggested relation: implemented_by.
